title: populus jacquemontiana var. glauca
intro: Populus jacquemontiana var. glauca, also known as Himalayan silver poplar or blue-leafed poplar, is a deciduous tree native to the Himalayan region. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Populus jacquemontiana var. glauca include reducing fever, relieving cough and cold, and treating digestive issues, as well as having antioxidant and anti-aging properties. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusion, decoction, and powder, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and capsules to leverage its medicinal benefits.
